generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance BuildingEnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time sensor capturing environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, air quality) within building infrastructure for DT synchronization via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralHealthSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor network monitoring structural integrity metrics (vibration, strain, deflection) for building components, integrated through Eclipse Hono for real-time DT updates"
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralDataTransmitted
]

instance EquipmentPerformanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled sensors tracking operational parameters of building systems (HVAC, lighting, elevators) with data ingestion via Eclipse Hono for DT state management"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EquipmentDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancyBehaviorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensors capturing occupancy patterns (motion, presence, usage intensity) to feed agent-based simulations in the DT framework"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingEnvironmentalSensor
]

instance StructuralDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthSensor
]

instance EquipmentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EquipmentPerformanceSensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancyBehaviorSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimDesignModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically rich 3D BIM model encapsulating geometric, spatial, and functional characteristics of building components with IFC standard compliance"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToDtTransformationEnabler, SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance OperationalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated runtime data from sensors and operational logs representing current building performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted, EquipmentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeMonitoringEnabler, PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance StructuralAnalysisData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structural health metrics derived from sensor networks and finite element analysis for failure prediction"
    DTDFVocab:fromData StructuralDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance OccupancyPatternData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed occupancy behavior data used for space utilization optimization and energy management scenarios"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OptimizationEnabler
]

instance EnergySimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based thermal model (EnergyPlus/OpenModelica) simulating building energy performance for optimization scenarios"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OptimizationEnabler
]

instance AgentBasedSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Agent-based model representing building system interactions and occupancy behavior for dynamic scenario analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BimToDtTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven automation framework converting BIM models to deployable DT artifacts with semantic preservation"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based and agent-based simulation engines (EnergyPlus, OpenModelica) for performance analysis and scenario testing"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware platform (OPC UA/MQTT) enabling continuous bidirectional data exchange between physical assets and DT via Eclipse Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AI/ML toolkit (TensorFlow/PyTorch) for anomaly detection and remaining useful life estimation using time-series databases"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance OptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization solvers (Gurobi/CPLEX) for resource allocation and energy management with multi-objective capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D/4D/5D visualization platform supporting AR/VR integration and collaborative workspaces"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance DataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Graph database (Neo4j) and time-series storage (InfluxDB) for complex relationship modeling and high-frequency sensor data management"
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized extension points enabling DT Engineers to develop domain-specific plugins and services"
    DTDFVocab:enables DecisionSupportService
]

instance LowCodeDevelopmentPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Rapid prototyping environment (Node-RED, Siemens MindSphere) for custom DT workflow automation"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregates and visualizes runtime data (sensor readings, environmental metrics) for operational oversight with threshold-based alerts"
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingOperationalInsight, MaintenanceAlertAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Forecasts asset degradation and failure probabilities using historical and real-time data with machine learning models"
    DTDFVocab:provides FailureProbabilityInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Applies simulation and optimization algorithms to improve energy efficiency and resource allocation through dynamic control signals"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight, ControlSignalAction
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides data-driven insights for strategic decisions (refurbishment planning, adaptive reuse) through scenario modeling"
    DTDFVocab:provides StrategicDecisionInsight, RefurbishmentRecommendationAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offers immersive 3D/4D/5D representations integrating time and cost dimensions for stakeholder alignment"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualAlignmentInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingOperationalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational state of building systems derived from synchronized sensor data and BIM metadata"
]

instance FailureProbabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted failure probabilities for building components based on structural health and equipment performance data"
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized energy performance metrics derived from simulation scenarios and real-time consumption data"
]

instance StrategicDecisionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven recommendations for long-term building lifecycle decisions including refurbishment and adaptive reuse"
]

instance VisualAlignmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Immersive visualization of building operations enabling intuitive monitoring and collaborative decision-making"
]

instance MaintenanceAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alerts triggered by threshold breaches in operational parameters"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance recommendations with remaining useful life estimates"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ControlSignalAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control signals generated for HVAC, lighting, and other building systems based on optimization results"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RefurbishmentRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Strategic recommendations for building refurbishment and system upgrades derived from lifecycle analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance BuildingControlSystemActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuators receiving control signals from DT optimization services to adjust building systems (HVAC, lighting, elevators)"
]

instance DigitalTwinFrameworkHub : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Central coordination component managing synchronization between decentralized DT instances and ensuring consistent data exchange"
]

// C10: Additional Models/Data
instance IotDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw IoT data ingested via Eclipse Hono from distributed sensor networks across building infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted, StructuralDataTransmitted, EquipmentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance BimMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Semantic metadata extracted from BIM models including geometric, spatial, and functional attributes of building components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToDtTransformationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Archived operational data from building systems used for trend analysis and predictive modeling"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

// C11: Additional Enablers
instance CpsIntegrationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cyber-Physical System architecture bridging static BIM models with runtime IoT data streams"
]

instance FederatedLearningEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Collaborative analytics framework preserving data sovereignty while enabling cross-stakeholder insights"
    DTDFVocab:enables DecisionSupportService
]

instance EdgeComputingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge processing infrastructure for low-latency sensor data handling in resource-constrained environments"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system enables **real-time control commands** from the digital twin to the physical building infrastructure, executed via a **centralized DT Framework** acting as a bidirectional hub. **Command types** include **operational adjustments** (e.g., HVAC setpoints, lighting controls, or structural monitoring triggers) and **device-specific directives** (e.g., actuator activations or sensor recalibrations). Control messages are formatted to align with **standardized extension points** of the modular architecture, ensuring compatibility with integrated services. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are implied by the framework’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which prioritize **scalability** and **maintainability**, suggesting role-based access control (RBAC) or cryptographic verification for command integrity. **Feedback systems** are supported through **bidirectional data exchange**, where executed commands generate **status confirmations** (e.g., success/failure acknowledgments) or **performance telemetry** (e.g., post-adjustment sensor readings). **Conflict resolution** is managed via the **runtime configuration management** feature, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment, while **command execution confirmation** is ensured through the **holistic life-cycle management** approach, where Facility Managers interact with the deployed DT for real-time monitoring and control."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, implying sub-second to millisecond-level latency requirements for critical operational data (e.g., sensor readings, control signals). The framework supports **dynamic temporal granularity** tailored to service-specific needs, such as high-frequency updates for monitoring (e.g., environmental sensors) and lower-frequency adjustments for configuration management. Runtime configuration management enables **on-demand temporal adjustments** without full redeployment, ensuring adaptability to evolving latency or synchronization demands. The system bridges static BIM representations with dynamic operational needs, implying a **multi-scale temporal hierarchy** where real-time synchronization coexists with periodic batch updates for analytics or long-term trend analysis."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **service-oriented architecture (SOA) pattern**, where the **DT Framework** acts as a **central orchestration hub** integrating heterogeneous components through **standardized extension points**. Key architectural roles include:
  - **Bidirectional data exchange** between physical systems (e.g., IoT sensors, building automation) and digital counterparts, enabled by **real-time synchronization protocols**.
  - **Modular service integration**, allowing *DT Engineers* to extend capabilities via custom analytics or control services.
  - **Runtime configuration management**, enabling dynamic adjustments to system behavior (e.g., reconfiguring data flows, adding/removing services) without full redeployment.
  The constellation leverages **coordinated roles** (*Building Control Engineers*, *Facility Managers*, *DT Engineers*) to manage the DT lifecycle, from **BIM-enriched model deployment** to **operational monitoring**. Resource management is implicit in the **scalability requirements**, ensuring the system handles growth from individual components to entire infrastructure systems while maintaining **low-overhead maintainability** and **accessibility** for diverse user expertise levels."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in this model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **runtime operation**, addressing the gap between static BIM representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** within the BIM environment, creating a **semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase leverages **enriched BIM models** to represent Building Control System (BCS) components, which are otherwise unsupported by standard BIM tools.
  2. **Model Transformation**: The framework employs **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles** to extract **structured representations** from enriched BIM models, enabling **automated generation of deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention. This phase ensures seamless transition from static BIM to dynamic DT representations.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, establishing a **real-time synchronized** connection between the physical building and its digital twin. This phase supports **immersive visualization** for monitoring and control, facilitated by interactive interfaces.
  4. **Runtime Operation**: The DT operates continuously, with **real-time data exchange** ensuring synchronization between physical and digital counterparts. Facility Managers interact with the twin to monitor system states and operate infrastructure, while DT Engineers extend functionality via **custom services and plugins**.
  **Lifecycle Governance**: The framework’s modular architecture includes **standardized extension points**, enabling integration of new services and analytics while maintaining interoperability. Version control and evolution management are implied through the **modular design**, allowing DTs to adapt to changing operational requirements without disrupting existing workflows."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT system emphasizes **model fidelity** through the integration of **BIM-based design-time models** with **runtime operational data**, ensuring alignment between static design representations and dynamic real-world conditions. Validation and verification methods are implied through the **model-driven automation framework**, which facilitates reproducible DT setups and reduces manual intervention (via **R2**). Uncertainty sources are mitigated by **real-time synchronization** (via **R3**), though explicit quantification methods or error correction mechanisms are not detailed. Quality assurance processes are supported by the **modular architecture** (via **R4**), enabling scalable and interoperable DT development, while the **Design Science Research (DSR)** methodology underpinning the framework ensures systematic validation of artifacts against domain-specific requirements (e.g., semantic integration, real-time connectivity)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this DT system is designed to support **heterogeneous interoperability** across physical and digital components, with a focus on **low-latency, high-reliability communication**. While specific protocols are not explicitly named, the framework’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** imply compatibility with **industry-standard protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or REST APIs) for device integration and data exchange. The **network topology** is likely **hybrid**, combining **edge computing nodes** (for localized processing) with a **central cloud/on-premise hub** (for global coordination). **Bandwidth requirements** are optimized for real-time synchronization, with **latency constraints** aligned to operational criticality (e.g., sub-100ms for control loops). **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are addressed through the **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, emphasizing **scalability** and **maintainability**, suggesting failover protocols, load balancing, and distributed data storage. **Security protocols** for network communication are implied by the framework’s emphasis on **accessibility for users with varying technical expertise**, likely incorporating **encryption (TLS/SSL), authentication (OAuth/JWT), and role-based access control (RBAC)** to safeguard data integrity and confidentiality."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for this DT system supports a **multi-tiered hosting model**, combining **cloud, edge, and on-premise** platforms to balance computational demands, latency, and data sovereignty. **Computational requirements** are met through a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling dynamic service integration and scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems. **Storage requirements** are optimized for **enriched BIM models** and operational data, with **scalability mechanisms** (e.g., auto-scaling, distributed storage) ensuring seamless handling of growing datasets. The framework supports **runtime configuration management**, allowing **low-overhead updates** and **adjustments without full redeployment**, which implies **containerized or microservice-based deployment models** (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes). **Availability and reliability** are assured through the **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which prioritize **maintainability** and **interoperability**, suggesting **high-availability clustering, backup/recovery systems, and geographically redundant deployments**. The **DT life cycle** is managed via coordinated roles (e.g., Building Control Engineers for deployment, Facility Managers for monitoring), ensuring alignment with **operational needs** throughout the building’s lifecycle."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** through **standardized data exchange protocols** and **modular integration patterns**. Key integration aspects include:
  - **Bidirectional data exchange** with external systems (e.g., BIM tools for device modeling, IoT platforms for sensor data, enterprise systems for facility management).
  - **Dependencies on interoperability standards** (implied by the requirement to bridge static BIM representations with dynamic operational needs) to ensure seamless communication between **AECO domain** tools (e.g., Autodesk Revit, Siemens Building Information Modeling) and real-time control systems.
  - **Integration with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems** via the **DT Framework’s extension points**, enabling **federated architectures** where multiple DT instances collaborate while maintaining autonomy. The system supports **enterprise-level integration** by addressing **scalability** (from components to infrastructure) and **accessibility** for users with varying technical backgrounds, ensuring compatibility with legacy and modern systems alike."
]
